name: Weimaraner
description: The Weimaraner, nicknamed the Gray Ghost for its distinctive silvery coat and pale amber or blue-gray eyes, was developed at the Weimar court in early 19th-century Germany as an all-purpose dog for tracking large game including boar, bear, and deer. As big game became scarce they transitioned to bird hunting, and today they are energetic, intelligent sporting dogs that require substantial daily exercise or they become destructive indoors. They are intensely devoted to their families and suffer significant separation anxiety, making them best suited to households where someone is home much of the day.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Hypothyroidism
conditions_detail.description: Hypothyroidism is the most common endocrine disorder in dogs, caused by reduced thyroid hormone production — most often from immune-mediated thyroiditis or idiopathic follicular atrophy. It affects metabolism, skin, and coat quality.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Daily oral thyroxine (levothyroxine) supplementation is highly effective and required for life. Dosing is adjusted based on thyroid level monitoring every 6-12 months.
prevention_tips: Regular blood screening in at-risk breeds from age 4

conditions_detail.name: Entropion
conditions_detail.description: Entropion is an inward rolling of the eyelid margin, causing the eyelashes and skin to rub against the corneal surface. This produces chronic irritation, pain, corneal ulceration, and scarring that can impair vision.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Temporary eyelid tacking can relieve discomfort in puppies pending maturity. Definitive surgical correction (Hotz-Celsus procedure) is highly effective in adults.
prevention_tips: Avoid breeding dogs with severe conformational entropion
Do not breed surgically corrected dogs
